name: Paisaje Protegido Serra del Maigmó y Serra del Sit — Circular por el Balcón de Alicante
region_intro: Mountain biking around Agost, located in the province of Alicante, Spain, features diverse natural landscapes. The region is characterized by significant mountain ranges, including the Sierra del Maigmó and Sierra del Cid, offering varied terrain. Riders will encounter eroded badlands, areas of pine forest, and agricultural lands with olive groves and vineyards. The network of trails includes greenways that follow former railway tracks, providing a mix of challenging climbs and more accessible paths. Best mountain bike trails around Agost The most popular mountain bike trail is Paisaje Protegido Serra del Maigmó y Serra del Sit — Circular por el Balcón de Alicante, a difficult 32.9 miles (53.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views from the Balcón de Alicante. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Puig Campana – Canteras Trail loop from Agost, a moderate 36.7 miles (59.1 km) path. This route provides extensive views and varied terrain through the landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the El Maigmó Greenway – Rest Area by the Reservoir loop from Agost, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde del Maigmó, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Agost is defined by mountainous terrain, greenways, and agricultural valleys, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Agost are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Agost's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Agost?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Agost, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 1000 mountain biking tours in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Agost?
A: Mountain biking around Agost features diverse terrain, from the eroded landscapes known as badlands and areas of pine forest to agricultural lands with olive groves and vineyards. You'll also find trails that follow former railway tracks, like the Vía Verde del Maigmó, and routes through the Sierra del Maigmó and Sierra del Cid mountains.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Agost?
A: Yes, Agost offers easy mountain bike trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14647112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó Greenway – Rest Area by the Reservoir loop from Agost&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 miles (21.8 km) long and follows the Vía Verde del Maigmó, making it suitable for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Agost?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider routes that venture into the Sierra del Maigmó and Sierra del Cid mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925758942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paisaje Protegido Serra del Maigmó y Serra del Sit — Circular por el Balcón de Alicante&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.9 miles (53.0 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views from the Balcón de Alicante.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Agost?
A: Yes, many routes around Agost are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14668221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana – Canteras Trail loop from Agost&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.7 miles (59.1 km) long and provides extensive views and varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Agost offer views of significant natural features. You can encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó and Sit Mountains&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of a protected forest core. Other notable points include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3072247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Silla del Cid Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almortxó Sand Dunes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Agost?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from greenways to mountainous terrain, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Agost?
A: Agost's climate is conducive to year-round outdoor activity. However, to avoid the intense summer heat, the best times for mountain biking are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November), when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27644187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Hermitage of San Pedro loop from Agost&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.5 km route that allows you to explore these historical landmarks while enjoying the ride.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails in Agost?
A: Agost is an inland town in Alicante province. While local buses might connect to nearby towns, direct public transport to specific trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the starting points of the trails.

Q: Are there places to park near the mountain bike trails in Agost?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes start from or pass through Agost town, where you can typically find public parking. For specific trailheads outside the town, look for designated parking areas or consult local maps for suitable spots.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Agost?
A: Agost town offers various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. For routes that venture further out, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, as amenities might be sparse along the trails themselves.
tips: Honoring its name, from the balcony you will have the best views of the city of Alicante and the beautiful beaches of the Costa Blanca.
